skill.name: seo-audit
skill.description: Diagnose SEO issues affecting crawlability, indexation, rankings, and organic performance. The skill produces evidence-based findings, health scoring, and prioritized recommendations without changing the site.
skill.summary: Audit SEO health and prioritize technical, content, and on-page improvements.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but the reviewed lines are markdown SEO guidance, checklist text, or formulas. No executable code, external command invocation, credential access, network request, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempt was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Reported as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution. Line 99 is inline markdown around the SEO directive noindex, and lines 295-298 are a fenced scoring formula. No shell command, Ruby code, or user-controlled command execution is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax appears only in markdown formatting and a formula block. There is no executable language context or command invocation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: SEO Text Reported as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns on SEO description, checklist, and scoring lines. These lines contain ordinary SEO terminology and markdown headings, not cryptographic APIs, hashing functions, encryption calls, or password handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are natural-language audit guidance. I found no evidence of cryptographic implementation or insecure cryptographic use.

low_findings.title: False Positive: SEO Audit Vocabulary Reported as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged system and network reconnaissance terms. The referenced lines discuss sitemap accessibility, valid certificates, mobile-first indexing, scoring bands, change tracking, and action-plan notes. These are SEO audit concepts, not host discovery, port scanning, or network probing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged wording is embedded in an SEO checklist and output format. No scanning commands, network tooling, IP enumeration, or target discovery instructions were found.

content.user_title: Audit SEO Health and Prioritize Fixes
content.value_statement: SEO problems are hard to prioritize when data comes from many tools and reports. This skill turns technical, on-page, content, and trust signals into clear findings, scores, and action priorities.
actual_capabilities: Reviews crawlability, indexation, technical SEO, on-page optimization, content quality, and authority signals.
Produces an SEO Health Index with weighted category scores and health bands.
Requires each issue to include evidence, severity, confidence, impact, and recommendation.
Builds a prioritized action plan from findings and score impact.
Supports comparison with prior audits when previous scores and findings are available.
Identifies when related skills may be useful after audit findings are accepted.
limitations: Does not guarantee ranking improvements or traffic gains.
Depends on provided evidence from crawlers, analytics, Search Console, or page samples.
Does not execute website changes unless the user separately requests implementation.
Authority and competition signals are directional unless external data is supplied.

use_cases.title: Diagnose a Traffic Decline
use_cases.description: Review technical, indexation, content, and authority signals to identify likely causes of organic traffic loss.

use_cases.title: Prioritize Site Migration Issues
use_cases.description: Evaluate redirects, canonical tags, sitemap coverage, metadata, and crawl signals after a redesign or CMS change.

use_cases.title: Improve Content and Page Quality
use_cases.description: Assess page intent, headings, titles, internal links, and trust signals before revising high-value pages.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Page Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one page or small section.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this page for SEO issues. Focus on title, meta description, headings, content intent, internal links, and obvious technical problems. Provide evidence and prioritized recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Technical SEO Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review using crawler or Search Console evidence.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the provided crawl and Search Console data. Identify crawlability, indexation, canonical, redirect, sitemap, mobile, and Core Web Vitals issues. Include severity, confidence, and score impact.

prompt_templates.title: Full SEO Health Index
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced audit with scoring and action planning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a full SEO audit with an SEO Health Index. Score crawlability, technical foundations, on-page optimization, content quality, and authority signals. Derive the action plan directly from findings.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Audit Progress
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced follow-up after fixes or a prior audit.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare this audit data against the previous audit. Show score changes, explain which fixes caused movement, identify unresolved blockers, and update the prioritized action plan.

output_examples.input: Audit a SaaS homepage using the provided crawl export and PageSpeed notes.
output: Executive summary with the main SEO constraints.
SEO Health Index with category scores and weighted contributions.
Prioritized findings with evidence, severity, confidence, score impact, and recommendations.
Action plan grouped by critical blockers, high-impact improvements, quick wins, and longer-term opportunities.

output_examples.input: Review why product category pages are not appearing in search results.
output: Indexation diagnosis covering robots rules, noindex signals, canonical conflicts, sitemap inclusion, and internal linking.
Evidence table based on submitted URLs or crawl data.
Likely root causes ranked by confidence and expected impact.

output_examples.input: Recheck an SEO audit after the development team fixed redirects and metadata.
output: Updated score delta with changes attributed to verified fixes.
Remaining issues that still limit the score.
Revised action priorities based on unresolved evidence.

best_practices: Provide crawl exports, Search Console data, analytics context, and representative URLs when available.
State the business goal, target market, and audit scope before requesting a full review.
Validate important findings with more than one evidence source when possible.
anti_patterns: Ask for ranking guarantees or exact traffic forecasts from the audit.
Treat a tool score as the finding without interpreting the underlying data.
Request implementation steps before confirming the audit findings and priorities.

faq.question: Does this skill make SEO changes automatically?
faq.answer: No. It identifies issues, explains impact, scores SEO health, and recommends priorities. Implementation requires a separate request.

faq.question: What data should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide URLs, crawl data, Search Console exports, analytics context, PageSpeed results, and known recent site changes.

faq.question: Can it audit a single page?
faq.answer: Yes. It can review one page, a section, or a full site when enough evidence is available.

faq.question: How does the SEO Health Index work?
faq.answer: It scores five SEO categories with fixed weights and deducts points based on issue severity and confidence.

faq.question: Does it replace an SEO crawler?
faq.answer: No. It interprets evidence from crawlers and other tools. It does not replace data collection.

faq.question: Can it compare audits over time?
faq.answer: Yes. If prior findings or scores are available, it can report score changes and explain which fixes moved the result.
